title: Capital Markets, Treasury Finance
basic: 5+ years of experience in debt capital markets, including structuring and executing ABS financing transactions (e.g., warehouse facilities, loan sales, securitizations) from a role at an issuer, bank, lender, or similar institution.
Bachelor’s degree.
Strong analytical skills with the ability to leverage data insights into a compelling narrative.
Excellent communication and cross-functional collaboration skills.
Creative and critical thinking abilities in a fast-paced, often ambiguous environment.
Project management skills with high attention to detail.
bonus: Experience with SQL or Python.
Background in managing liquidity investments.

title: Financing & Capital Risk, Treasury Finance
basic: 7+ years of experience in Treasury, transaction/product pricing, financial risk management, or capital/liquidity unit economics
Proven ability to integrate fundamental analysis with applied risk management in areas like product pricing, credit/funding valuation, or portfolio management
Strong self-management skills to handle both high-velocity, short-term deliverables and long-term strategic objectives
Demonstrated curiosity, resilience, and problem-solving skills for a dynamic, fast-paced team environment
Excellent verbal and written communication skills
bonus: Experience with SQL or a willingness to learn
A builder's mindset with an inclination to solve problems systematically via infrastructure, automation, and repeatable frameworks
Ability to thrive in an unstructured, ambiguous, and fast-moving environment with a 'roll up your sleeves' attitude

title: Financial Analyst, FP&A
basic: 5+ years of experience in FP&A or corporate finance
Strong understanding of corporate finance concepts and accounting principles
Advanced proficiency in spreadsheet software (MS Excel/Google Sheets)
Experience with ERP software (e.g., Anaplan)
Proven ability to turn data into actionable insights and present complex financial analyses to senior management
Strong cross-functional collaboration, communication, planning, and organizational skills
Ability to work effectively in a dynamic and uncertain environment
bonus: A degree in finance, accounting, economics, or a related quantitative field
Curiosity and willingness to learn and leverage AI tools and emerging technologies

title: Corporate Finance Analyst II, General & Administrative
basic: Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Business, Economics, or a related field
2-4 years of experience in financial planning, analysis, or operations with a strong track record of managing Opex
Proficiency in financial modeling and tools like Excel or Google Sheets
Experience building and managing budgets, forecasts, and variance analyses
Strong organizational skills, attention to detail, and ability to manage priorities in a fast-paced environment
Clear communication and presentation skills for cross-functional stakeholders
bonus: Experience with financial planning systems such as Adaptive Insights
Experience with data visualization tools (e.g., Power BI) and an interest in financial process automation
Prior experience specifically in FP&A, operating expense management, or a Corporate Finance environment
Familiarity with process improvement and building efficiency through financial systems
Demonstrated ability to translate complex financial data into actionable insights for non-finance teams

title: Corporate Finance Analyst, Research & Development
basic: Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Accounting, Business, Economics, or a related field
2-4 years of experience in financial planning, analysis, or operations with a strong track record of managing Opex
Proficiency in financial modeling and spreadsheet software (e.g., Excel, Google Sheets)
Experience building and managing budgets, forecasts, and variance analyses
Strong organizational skills, attention to detail, and ability to manage competing priorities in a fast-paced environment
Clear communication and presentation skills with experience presenting insights to cross-functional stakeholders
A self-starter with a positive attitude who thrives in a dynamic, team-oriented setting
bonus: Experience with financial planning systems such as Adaptive Insights
Proficiency with data visualization tools (e.g., Power BI) and an interest in process automation
Prior experience specifically in FP&A, operating expense management, or a Corporate Finance environment
Familiarity with process improvement and building efficiencies through financial systems
Demonstrated ability to translate complex financial data into actionable insights for both finance and non-finance audiences

title: Marketing Financial Analyst
basic: 5+ years experience in Finance, Business, Marketing, or a related field
Experience owning and evolving financial operations, including budgeting, spend governance, and performance forecasting
Expertise in financial modeling and reporting with strong analytical skills to derive insights from complex data
Knowledge of marketing finance concepts and frameworks
Proven track record of designing and implementing process improvement projects
Strong project management, organizational skills, and attention to detail
Ability to work independently, solve problems, and manage conflicting stakeholder requirements
bonus: Experience working in a marketplace business
Experience with Adaptive Insights or similar financial planning systems
Familiarity with commercial planning, including forecasting and investment strategy
Understanding of Marketing measurement frameworks, analytics, and performance tracking
